lanes.objective: Extract a reusable product-family kernel from the closed homepage and /imc laws, and separate flagship-only behavior from generic dossier law.
acceptance: kernel law artifact exists
generic /work/[slug] law is explicit
flagship exceptions are separated from family law

lanes.objective: Verify that XR and FT packets satisfy the minimum binding requirements for generic product routes without presentation-time fabrication.
acceptance: packet bindings remain honest
fallback rules are explicit

lanes.objective: Define and validate the falsification bundle for the product-family kernel before rollout starts.
acceptance: kernel QA bundle is declared
generic route promotion rules are explicit

lanes.objective: Run one bounded system-improvement ratchet against the GGD and CLAW machine, converting recurring XR and autonomy failures into stricter laws, tighter prompts, better evaluators, or safer runner behavior without touching route implementation files.
acceptance: writable scope stayed inside declared system paths
system eval bundle is clean after the slice
the change is either measurably better and kept or truthfully rejected with learning captured

lanes.objective: Verify that the XR packet contains fields sufficient to populate all content sections of the Stitch product page template (hero, metrics grid, proof assertions, evidence routes, repo shape, related lanes). Document any coverage gaps as non-blocking issues. Do NOT block on exhaustive GitHub truth surface coverage — focus on Stitch template field sufficiency.
acceptance: XR packet contains data sufficient to populate each Stitch template section, under any field naming convention. The known mapping is: name=laneTitle, tagline=tagline, status=authorityState.status, metrics=headlineMetrics, proofAssertions=provedNow, repoShape=repoShape, evidenceRoutes=verificationPath OR proofAnchors. relatedLanes is optional for initial proof.
XR packet freshness verified against GitHub main HEAD via commitSha field
Any coverage gaps documented as non-blocking notes in the handoff JSON, not in committed report files

lanes.objective: STITCH RESCAFFOLD: Read the Google Stitch product page HTML prototype from /Users/zer0palab/Zer0pa Website/Zer0pa ZPE Product Pages/ and rebuild /work/[slug]/page.tsx to match its layout structure. Wire dynamic content from loadLaneBySlug() packet cache. Use placeholder divs for visualization widget spaces. See CLAW/control-plane/directives/stitch-rescaffold.json for full instructions.
acceptance: XR renders from the kernel
XR remains packet-truthful

lanes.objective: Run falsification against /work/xr and reject any kernel, proportionality, or responsive regression.
acceptance: XR has zero critical and zero major layout diffs
XR has no blocking geometry-law failure

lanes.objective: Replay the accepted XR candidate through the integration lane before promotion.
acceptance: XR candidate replays cleanly in integration

acceptance: sub-agent returned accepted or hold with truthful report
writable scope stayed inside declared opus-engineer paths
sub-agent committed if any files changed
